channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19297] sanity-sec test_72b: test_72b returned 1
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/411a89ee-bb83-476b-8e1e-6bc4c5557cb6

test_72b failed with the following error:

test_72b returned 1

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/115980 - 5.15.0-142-generic
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/115980 - 4.18.0-553.58.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

Failed twice on unrelated patches in the last two days. Likely due to recent nodemap/fileset changes.

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity-sec test_72b - test_72b returned 1

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
More of the same flavor on other testsets in

  • sanity-lnet https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/47b64eed-35eb-49cc-a45d-e7b5dd2c72f0
  • sanity-flr https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/9861dd97-760b-4c83-b3ee-18f7d4de0a12

adilger These failures don't seem to be code-related, and it looks like to be another flavor of the TIMEOUT network failures in Ubuntu tests we have been seeing for a while (LU-18569). There are many more of those failures for Ubuntu all across sanity-sec (and only Ubuntu). 3 more for reference in addition to the one you saw from the past weeks, and they are all similar:

  • trevis: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/15bcc7c2-6292-47b0-8eab-37300bbd6b2f
  • onyx: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/b85f16a8-3fef-4803-acd5-3dc263147794
  • trevis: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/07da5722-d068-4bca-a453-dabc9edba8fa

What is happening is that pdsh fails, e.g., during "do_node()".

All of these tests have the following lines in the log:

pdsh@trevis-76vm4: trevis-76vm5: mcmd: connect failed: No route to host
pdsh@trevis-76vm4: trevis-76vm5: mcmd: connect failed: No route to host
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 60 secs left ...
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 55 secs left ...
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 50 secs left ...
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 45 secs left ...
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 40 secs left ...
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 35 secs left ...
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 30 secs left ...
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 25 secs left ...
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 20 secs left ...
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 15 secs left ...
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 10 secs left ...
waiting ping -c 1 -w 3 trevis-76vm5, 5 secs left ...
02:00:02 (1755050402) waited for trevis-76vm5 network 60s

There seem to be 3 retries, and if the third fails the test fails with "1". There are cases where it only failed once and continued:

  • https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/83f6a52c-2746-45b1-ae0b-efbe5b38a328

Once the error occurs, all consecutive testsets seem to have the same issue and fail.

Li Xi reported this error already in January https://jira.whamcloud.com/browse/DCO-9992 but was closed as not reproduced.

What is interesting is that this happens on both Onyx and Trevis, so this could just be some network config issue on the Ubuntu clients? Maybe a rate limiting of sorts that rejects further connections on the client? I've noticed that it is more likely to occur during client re-mounts (but not always).

It looks like a range of sanity-sec subtests are failing in the same way - from test_69 through test_77. It looks like something is returning an error in a stack_trap cleanup function, or similar? It might only be failing on ubuntu2204 sessions as well, based on a sample of 2.

mvef can you please take a look. Normally I wouldn't file a new ticket after 2 failures, but the patches seem totally unrelated to nodemaps, and the nodemap/fileset code that has been changing a lot recently, so better to catch it quickly.
